.factory/services.yaml.reason: Mission command wiring now runs through bash -lc so the manifest can source .factory/init.sh and invoke the Docker lane reliably.

android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh.reason: Runnable Docker lane script under review, including accepted-warning notes, analytics seeding, AAPT2 override wiring, and in-process Kotlin compilation used by the stabilized validators.

android/scripts/docker-gradle.Dockerfile.reason: Container image wiring paired with the stabilized script, including the copied ARM64 AAPT2 override and pre-seeded /home/gradle/.android analytics settings.

scopeStatement.truthfulClaim: This artifact intentionally cites the current broader Docker-lane stabilization state because the reviewed unit-test-lane fix is not isolated to a single unrelated commit. The claimed state includes manifest command wrapping, container analytics/home seeding, AAPT2 override wiring, and Kotlin in-process execution needed for the green Docker validators.
reviewedBehavior: commands.test, commands.typecheck, and commands.lint in .factory/services.yaml invoke .factory/init.sh through bash -lc before running android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh.
android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh documents the accepted non-blocking metrics/analytics warning and forces in-process Kotlin compilation for the Docker validator lane.
android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh seeds writable Android home state, mounts it into the container, and passes -Dcom.android.tools.analyticsOptOut plus the ARM64 AAPT2 override to Gradle.
android/scripts/docker-gradle.Dockerfile copies the ARM64 static AAPT2 override into /tmp/android-aapt2/aapt2 and pre-seeds /home/gradle/.android/analytics.settings in the image.
notClaimed: This artifact does not pretend commit 18f309245c4aedf13b0a0b7b76728decf2410771 contains the reviewed Docker lane state.
This artifact does not claim BrowserStack or release-packaging behavior.

verificationInterpretation.exactArtifactBoundary: The validators were run against the same workspace file set hashed above.
verificationInterpretation.reasonThisIsTruthful: The hashed files are the actual live Docker-lane state under review, and the verification commands exercised that exact state without citing an unrelated immutable commit.
